entry.title: Appcircle
entry.description: Appcircle is a mobile CI/CD platform for building, testing, and distributing iOS and Android apps in the cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 concurrent build, 30 build minutes per month, and basic distribution features.
caveats: No macOS builds on free tier
Limited support for private repositories

useCases: Run basic CI/CD pipelines for mobile projects
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Appcircle account.
Connect your GitHub or Bitbucket repository.
Configure your build workflow for iOS or Android.
Start a build and monitor progress in the dashboard.
Distribute the built app to testers or download the artifact.
entry.whenToUse: Use Appcircle to automate building and distributing mobile apps when you need a simple, cloud-based CI/CD solution.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced build minutes, macOS builds, or deep integration with enterprise tools.
entry.body: Appcircle provides a cloud-based CI/CD platform focused on mobile app development, offering easy setup for automating builds and distributing apps to testers.

entry.title: Azure Automation
entry.description: Azure Automation provides cloud-based process automation, configuration management, and update management for Azure and hybrid environments.

freeTierDetails.summary: 500 minutes of job runtime and 500 configuration management nodes per month are free.
caveats: Overages are billed per minute and per node.
Some features may incur additional charges.
billingRiskNotes: Jobs or nodes beyond free tier are billed automatically.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create an Automation Account.
Add a Runbook or import a sample Runbook.
Configure authentication and permissions.
Start or schedule your Runbook.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to automate repetitive Azure or hybrid cloud tasks, such as resource provisioning or patch management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require automation outside Azure or need advanced orchestration features not supported by Azure Automation.
entry.body: Azure Automation enables users to automate, configure, and update Azure and hybrid resources, reducing manual effort and improving operational efficiency.

entry.title: CircleCI
entry.description: CircleCI is a cloud-based continuous integration and delivery platform for automating software builds, tests, and deployments.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 6,000 build minutes per month and up to 30,000 credits for public and private projects.
caveats: Additional usage requires paid plan
Some features restricted to paid tiers

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a CircleCI account using GitHub or Bitbucket.
Connect your repository to CircleCI.
Add a .circleci/config.yml file to your project.
Push changes to trigger your first build.
Monitor build status and logs in the CircleCI dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use CircleCI to automate testing and deployment for projects hosted on GitHub or Bitbucket with straightforward CI/CD needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced self-hosted runners, unlimited concurrency, or deep integration with non-supported VCS providers.
entry.body: CircleCI provides a user-friendly platform for automating builds, tests, and deployments, with a generous free tier suitable for small teams and open source projects.

entry.title: Cirrus CI
entry.description: Cirrus CI is a continuous integration service supporting multiple platforms and flexible configuration for open source and private projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public open source repositories with unlimited builds.
limits: Limited free usage for private repositories
caveats: Private repositories have limited free usage; paid plans required for higher usage
Some advanced features may require paid plan

useCases: Automated testing for open source projects
quickstartSteps: Sign in to Cirrus CI with your GitHub or GitLab account.
Enable Cirrus CI for your repository from the dashboard.
Add a .cirrus.yml configuration file to your project root.
Push changes to trigger your first build.
Monitor build status and logs in the Cirrus CI dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Cirrus CI for open source projects needing free, cross-platform CI/CD with GitHub or GitLab integration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require extensive private repository builds or advanced enterprise features without a paid plan.
entry.body: Cirrus CI offers a flexible, cross-platform continuous integration service with a generous free tier for open source projects and easy GitHub/GitLab integration.

entry.title: Cirun.io
entry.description: Cirun.io provides cloud-based ephemeral GPU runners for CI/CD pipelines and development workflows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers limited GPU runner hours for CI/CD and development.
limits: Up to 20 GPU hours per month
caveats: Requires GitHub or GitLab integration
Limited support for custom hardware

useCases: Test machine learning models in pipelines
Automate GPU-based build and test workflows
quickstartSteps: Sign up at cirun.io with your GitHub or GitLab account.
Connect your repository and authorize Cirun.io access.
Configure your CI/CD workflow to use Cirun GPU runners.
Push a commit to trigger a GPU-enabled pipeline job.
Monitor job status and GPU usage in the Cirun dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Cirun.io when you need GPU resources for CI/CD or development without managing hardware.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require persistent GPU servers or need more than the free tier's limited GPU hours.
avoidIf: require specific GPU models not supported
entry.body: Cirun.io offers ephemeral cloud GPU runners for CI/CD and development, enabling easy access to GPU resources without infrastructure management.

entry.title: Codeberg
entry.description: Codeberg is a free, open-source Git hosting platform with collaborative development tools.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited public and private repositories with collaborative features at no cost.
caveats: No commercial support
Storage subject to fair use
No built-in CI/CD

quickstartSteps: Go to https://codeberg.org/ and sign up for a free account.
Verify your email address to activate your account.
Create a new repository or import an existing one.
Clone your repository locally using Git.
Push your code and start collaborating with others.
entry.whenToUse: Use Codeberg when you need free, privacy-focused Git hosting for open-source or private projects with basic collaboration tools.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require integrated CI/CD, advanced enterprise features, or guaranteed commercial support.
entry.body: Codeberg offers a free, open-source alternative to commercial Git hosting, focusing on privacy, collaboration, and community-driven development for individuals and small teams.

entry.title: CodeMagic
entry.description: CodeMagic is a CI/CD tool for automating mobile app builds, tests, and deployments.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 500 build minutes per month with limited concurrency.
caveats: macOS builds may queue during peak times
Overages require paid plan

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a CodeMagic account using GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.
Connect your mobile app repository.
Configure your build pipeline using the UI or codemagic.yaml.
Set up environment variables and secrets if needed.
Start your first build and review build logs.
entry.whenToUse: Use CodeMagic to automate building, testing, and deploying mobile apps, especially for Flutter projects or small teams.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need unlimited build minutes, advanced enterprise features, or extensive parallel builds for large teams.
entry.body: CodeMagic provides a simple way to automate mobile app CI/CD, with a generous free tier ideal for individuals and small teams working on Flutter or cross-platform apps.

entry.title: Country-State-City Microservice API
entry.description: Provides a free REST API for retrieving country, state, and city data for use in applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free access to country, state, and city data via REST API.
caveats: No SLA or uptime guarantee
Data accuracy may vary

quickstartSteps: Visit the official API documentation page.
Review available endpoints for countries, states, and cities.
Make a GET request to the desired endpoint using your preferred HTTP client.
Parse the JSON response in your application.
Integrate the data into your forms or workflows.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need quick, free access to country, state, and city data for forms or simple location features.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require guaranteed uptime, advanced filtering, or official support for production-critical systems.
entry.body: Country-State-City Microservice API offers a simple, always-free REST API for retrieving hierarchical location data, ideal for forms and quick integrations.

entry.title: Elastic Load Balancing
entry.description: AWS Elastic Load Balancing automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets for high availability and fault tolerance.

freeTierDetails.summary: The AWS Free Tier includes 750 hours per month of load balancer usage and 15 GB data processing for the first 12 months.
caveats: Free Tier valid for 12 months after AWS account creation
Overages billed at standard rates
billingRiskNotes: Usage beyond free limits incurs charges
No hard cap; monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs

useCases: Distributing web traffic across multiple servers
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an AWS account and log in to the AWS Management Console.
Navigate to the EC2 dashboard and select 'Load Balancers' under 'Load Balancing'.
Click 'Create Load Balancer' and choose the appropriate load balancer type.
Configure listeners, target groups, and security settings as required.
Review and launch the load balancer, then register your targets (EC2 instances or IPs).
entry.whenToUse: Use Elastic Load Balancing to automatically distribute incoming traffic and increase availability for applications hosted on AWS.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need a free, always-on load balancer after 12 months or if your workloads are not hosted on AWS.
entry.body: AWS Elastic Load Balancing offers scalable, managed load balancing for applications on AWS, supporting multiple protocols and high availability with minimal configuration.

entry.title: Everystep-automation.com
entry.description: Everystep-automation.com offers free website and web application monitoring with scriptable browser automation.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes basic website monitoring and browser scripting for a limited number of checks.
caveats: Advanced features and more checks require paid plans
Support is limited on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free account on the Everystep-automation.com website.
Download and install the Everystep Recorder tool.
Record a browser script simulating your desired user flow.
Upload the script to your Everystep account.
Configure monitoring schedule and alert preferences.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need simple, scriptable website or web app monitoring with browser automation and basic alerting.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced integrations, high-frequency checks, or enterprise-grade SLA and support.
entry.body: Everystep-automation.com provides free browser-based monitoring and automation for websites and web applications, allowing users to record and schedule synthetic tests with basic alerting.

entry.title: Financial Data
entry.description: Provides free access to real-time and historical financial market data via APIs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers limited API requests for real-time and historical market data.
caveats: Premium endpoints and higher limits require paid plans
Some data feeds may be delayed

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free account at https://financialdata.net/.
Verify your email and log in to the dashboard.
Generate an API key from your account settings.
Review API documentation for endpoint usage.
Make your first API request using your key.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need free, basic access to real-time or historical financial data for prototyping or educational projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high request volumes, premium data feeds, or guaranteed real-time accuracy for production trading.
entry.body: Financial Data offers a free API for accessing real-time and historical market data, suitable for learning, prototyping, and non-commercial projects.

entry.title: Flat.social
entry.description: Flat.social enables interactive virtual spaces for meetings, events, and workshops with customizable rooms and playful features.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows unlimited events with up to 10 participants per event.
caveats: Advanced features require paid plans
Participant cap per event

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Flat.social account.
Create a new virtual room from your dashboard.
Customize your room layout and features.
Invite participants by sharing the event link.
Start your event and interact in real time.
entry.whenToUse: Use Flat.social for engaging, small-scale virtual meetings or workshops where interactivity and playful features are desired.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need to host large events with more than 10 participants or require advanced event management tools.
entry.body: Flat.social provides a playful, interactive platform for small virtual events and meetings, ideal for teams and communities seeking engaging online experiences.

entry.title: Framagit.org
entry.description: Framagit.org is a free, open-source Git repository hosting platform powered by GitLab for collaborative software development.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited public and private repositories with collaborative features at no cost.
limits: Storage subject to fair use policy
caveats: Intended for non-commercial and open-source projects
No SLA or guaranteed uptime
May restrict heavy usage
billingRiskNotes: No billing; excessive use may result in account restriction

quickstartSteps: Register for a free account at https://framagit.org.
Create a new project or import an existing Git repository.
Invite collaborators by adding their usernames or emails.
Push your code using Git remote URLs provided by Framagit.
Configure issues, merge requests, and CI/CD pipelines as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Framagit.org for hosting open-source or small team projects when you want a privacy-friendly, non-commercial GitLab alternative.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need enterprise support, commercial use, or guaranteed uptime, or if your project requires heavy CI/CD resources.
entry.body: Framagit.org offers a free, privacy-respecting GitLab instance for collaborative code hosting, ideal for open-source and non-commercial projects with moderate resource needs.

entry.title: Houndci.com
entry.description: HoundCI automatically reviews GitHub pull requests for style violations in your codebase.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public repositories on GitHub.
limits: Supports JavaScript, Ruby, Python, Swift, and more
caveats: Private repositories require a paid plan

useCases: Improving code consistency in open source projects
quickstartSteps: Sign in with your GitHub account at https://houndci.com/.
Authorize HoundCI to access your repositories.
Enable HoundCI for the desired public repository.
Merge the provided configuration file or use the default settings.
Open a pull request to see automated style comments from HoundCI.
entry.whenToUse: Use HoundCI to automate code style checks for public GitHub repositories and reduce manual review workload.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need private repository support for free or require deep custom linting beyond supported languages.
avoidIf: you need private repo support for free
entry.body: HoundCI provides automated code style review for GitHub pull requests, helping teams maintain consistent code quality in public repositories.

entry.title: IP.City
entry.description: IP.City provides fast, privacy-focused IP geolocation API services with a generous free tier for developers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10,000 API requests per month with no credit card required.
caveats: No SLA on free tier
Advanced features require paid plan

quickstartSteps: Go to https://ip.city and sign up for a free account.
Verify your email address to activate your account.
Log in and navigate to the dashboard to obtain your API key.
Review API documentation for endpoint usage.
Integrate the API key into your application to start making requests.
entry.whenToUse: Use IP.City when you need reliable, privacy-friendly IP geolocation for apps or websites with moderate monthly request volumes.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require guaranteed uptime, advanced features, or high request volumes beyond the free tier.
entry.body: IP.City offers a simple, privacy-focused IP geolocation API with a generous free tier, making it ideal for developers needing basic location data without complex setup or billing risk.

entry.title: Scrutinizer-ci.com
entry.description: Scrutinizer-ci.com provides automated code quality analysis and continuous integration for various programming languages.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public and open source repositories with unlimited analysis.
caveats: Private repositories are not included in the free tier
Some advanced features may require a paid plan

useCases: Automated code review for open source projects
Static code analysis to improve code quality
quickstartSteps: Sign up at scrutinizer-ci.com using your GitHub, Bitbucket, or GitLab account.
Authorize Scrutinizer to access your repositories.
Select a public repository to add to Scrutinizer.
Configure analysis settings or use the default configuration.
Trigger your first analysis by pushing code or manually starting a build.
entry.whenToUse: Use Scrutinizer-ci.com for automated code quality checks and CI on public or open source repositories.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need CI for private repositories without a paid plan or require advanced enterprise features.
avoidIf: need private repo support for free
entry.body: Scrutinizer-ci.com offers free, unlimited code analysis for public and open source repositories, making it ideal for students and open source maintainers seeking automated code quality checks.

entry.title: Traducciones.app
entry.description: Traducciones.app offers free, fast, and privacy-friendly online text translation for over 100 languages.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free text translations via web interface.
caveats: No advanced features like document translation or batch processing

quickstartSteps: Go to https://traducciones.app/ in your browser.
Select the source and target languages.
Enter or paste your text into the input box.
Click the translate button to view the result.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need fast, free, and privacy-friendly translation of short texts via a web browser.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need API access, document translation, or batch processing for large-scale or automated workflows.
entry.body: Traducciones.app is a free, privacy-focused online translation tool supporting over 100 languages, ideal for quick and simple text translations.
